title: genista carpetana subsp. nociva
intro: Genista carpetana subsp. nociva, also known as Spanish broom, is a shrub species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Spanish medicine, to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Genista carpetana subsp. nociva include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
